07 May 2026 Military News

News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • CENTCOM Protects U.S. Warships Transiting Strait of Hormuz CENTCOM 07 May 2026 -- U.S. forces intercepted unprovoked Iranian attacks and responded with self-defense strikes as U.S. Navy guided-missile destroyers transited the Strait of Hormuz to the Gulf of Oman, May 7.
  • US destroyers flee Strait of Hormuz after massive Iranian missile and drone barrage: IRGC Navy Press TV 07 May 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) Navy says its forces inflicted significant damage on US naval assets in a large-scale combined operation on Thursday evening, forcing three American destroyers to flee the Strait of Hormuz.
  • Qeshm Port Hit by Enemy Fire, Say Iranian Media RFE/RL 07 May 2026 -- Parts of Iran's Bahman Port on Qeshm Island had been targeted by unknown attackers, the semiofficial Fars News Agency reported late on May 7.

  • May 7: Iran swiftly responds to new US adventurism in Strait of Hormuz, reasserts dominance Press TV 07 May 2026 -- Iranian naval and missile forces delivered a swift, precise response to another act of US aggression in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday evening, forcing American forces to retreat after Washington struck an Iranian oil tanker in the Gulf of Oman.
  • Iranian forces strike US vessels in swift retaliation for tanker attack: Top military comm Press TV 07 May 2026 -- A spokesperson for the Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters said on Thursday that Iranian forces gave an immediate and decisive response to a series of US military aggressions in the strategic waters of the Strait of Hormuz and along the country's southern coastline.
  • Enemy strikes Iran's vital Qeshm Island pier; explosions heard in Bandar Abbas Press TV 07 May 2026 -- The Bahman Pier in the Qeshm island of Iran came under attack late on Thursday night during a fierce exchange of fire between Iranian armed forces and enemy units operating near the Strait of Hormuz, Iranian state media reported.
  • Iranian missiles force US aggressors to flee Strait of Hormuz after tanker attack Press TV 07 May 2026 -- Iranian naval and missile forces delivered a swift, precise, and powerful response to yet another reckless act of US aggression in the Persian Gulf, forcing enemy units to retreat in disarray after sustaining direct hits, according to official reports.
  • Two Iranian tankers break illegal US naval blockade: Satellite tracker Press TV 07 May 2026 -- A website tracking oil shipments around the world says two Iranian tankers have managed to pass through the illegal US blockade of the country's ports.
  • Iran Denies Involvement In South Korea Ship Incident RFE/RL 07 May 2026 -- In a statement, Tehran's embassy in Seoul has said that Iran wasn't involved in an incident that damaged a South Korean ship in the Strait of Hormuz.
  • Iran firmly rejects any role in South Korean ship explosion in Hormuz Strait Press TV 07 May 2026 -- Iran's embassy in Seoul has firmly rejected reports of involvement in an explosion that targeted a South Korean-linked Panama-flagged cargo ship — HMM Maru — in the Strait of Hormuz earlier this week.

  • Trump Says Europe Agrees Iran's Government 'Can Never Have A Nuclear Weapon' RFE/RL 07 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump says he spoke with the European Commission's president about Iran and ongoing trade negotiations with the European Union.
  • Trump halted 'Project Freedom' after backlash from Saudi Arabia: US officials Press TV 07 May 2026 -- Two US officials have revealed that President Donald Trump's decision to retreat from an earlier plan to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, known as "Project Freedom," came following significant backlash from its close Persian Gulf ally, Saudi Arabia.
  • Trump says U.S. had "very good" talks with Iran in past 24 hours People's Daily 07 May 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump on Wednesday said the United States has had very good talks over the past 24 hours with Iran on ending the war that has lasted more than two months.

  • Iran maritime authority says ready to provide services to commercial vessels operating in Strait of Hormuz Press TV 07 May 2026 -- Iran's Ports and Maritime Organization (PMO) has announced that it is fully prepared to offer maritime services, technical assistance, and medical support to commercial vessels navigating the strategic Strait of Hormuz and surrounding regional waters.
  • Tehran Reviews Latest US Proposal Amid Trump Optimism -- And Threats RFE/RL 07 May 2026 -- Iran said it was reviewing the latest US proposal that sources say would end the fighting but leave several key issues open, as US President Donald Trump claimed a deal was "very possible" but also warned of renewed fighting if Tehran rejected the deal.
  • Defense official: US must recognize Iran's rights, break with Israel to escape 'quagmire' Press TV 07 May 2026 -- The spokesperson for Iran's Ministry of Defense says the United States and the Israeli regime must recognize the rights of the Iranian nation to pave the way for putting an end to the war against the Islamic Republic.

  • Strait of Hormuz crisis: Fertilizer scarcity will affect next harvests and food supplies, FAO warns FAO 07 May 2026 -- The Director-General of the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), QU Dongyu, emphasized today that the global fertilizer scarcity caused by disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz will lead to lower yields and tightening food supplies in the latter half of 2026 and into 2027. He spoke at the Ministerial Meeting of the MED9++ Countries on "Supporting Food Security and Access to Fertilizers" co-chaired by FAO, Italy and Croatia.
  • Bahrain and US float Security Council resolution on the Strait of Hormuz UN News 07 May 2026 -- Bahrain and the United States have circulated a draft Security Council resolution calling for Iran to cease attacks in the Strait of Hormuz, their ambassadors outlined to journalists at UN Headquarters in New York on Thursday.

  • Executive Director meets with Prime Minister of Canada on energy market turmoil amid Middle East conflict International Energy Agency (IEA) 07 May 2026 -- Discussions with government leaders in Ottawa focus on impacts of Strait of Hormuz disruption and Canada's opportunity to become one of the world's major energy exporters

  • Barry Pavel: The US Strategy In Iran, And What Comes Next RFE/RL 07 May 2026 -- As the White House signals a pause in Project Freedom while insisting that a key phase of military operations against Iran has concluded, the broader trajectory of the conflict remains uncertain. Is this a genuine step toward de-escalation -- or merely a tactical lull before the next phase?

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Bumblebee V1 System Sharpens 10th Mountain Division's Counter-Drone Edge DoD News 07 May 2026 -- Soldiers assigned to the 10th Mountain Division began training with the Bumblebee V1 counter-drone system, introducing a capability to improve the warfighter's ability to detect and defeat the threat of unmanned aerial systems on the modern battlefield.
  • Tinker brings the B-1 back to the fight US Air Force 07 May 2026 -- A B-1B Lancer once parked in the Arizona desert is back in the air after an intensive regeneration and depot maintenance effort led by the Oklahoma City Air Logistics Complex at Tinker Air Force Base.
  • Coast Guard awards $400 million contract for historic Training Center Cape May enhancements US Coast Guard 07 May 2026 -- The U.S. Coast Guard has awarded a contract valued at up to $400 million to Whiting Turner Contracting Company for the design and construction of new facilities at Coast Guard Training Center (TRACEN) Cape May, New Jersey. This marks the largest shore construction award in Coast Guard history and is part of a broader effort to modernize the service's only accession point for enlisted personnel.
  • Inaugural drone academics held in Morocco during African Lion 26 Army News 07 May 2026 -- The inaugural African Lion drone academics class graduated more than 20 service members from four nations, trained on cost-effective small unmanned aircraft systems at Southern Zone Headquarters in Agadir, Morocco, May 5.
  • Senegal, US and allied medical teams conclude multinational medical readiness exercise during African Lion 26 Army News 07 May 2026 -- Military medical personnel representing the U.S., Senegal, Italy and Austria concluded a multinational medical readiness exercise in southern Senegal to end African Lion 26, May 8.

  • Data on interceptions of aircraft completed near the Baltic States' borders on April 26-May 3, 2026 Lithuania MND 07 May 2026

  • Meeting with Rostec CEO Sergei Chemezov President of Russia 07 May 2026 -- CEO of Rostec State Corporation Sergei Chemezov briefed the President on the company's performance in 2025, the implementation of the state defence order, and the development of civil aviation and automobile manufacturing, as well as on the development of socially significant digital solutions.
  • Statement by the Deputy Head of the Delegation of the Russian Federation at the 11th Review Conference of the Parties to the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons, Deputy Director of the Department for Non-Proliferation and Arms Control of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Russian Federation M.Kondratenkov (Cluster III, Peaceful uses of nuclear energy), New York, 5 May 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 07 May 2026

  • Norway to allocate approximately $302 million to a new PURL package to strengthen Ukraine's air defence Ukraine MOD 07 May 2026 -- Norway will allocate approximately $302 million to a new package under PURL (Prioritised Ukraine Requirements List), which will include, among other items, missiles for Patriot systems. This is an important decision aimed at strengthening Ukraine's air defence and protecting Ukrainian cities, critical infrastructure, and the positions of the Defence Forces of Ukraine.
  • Ivan Havryliuk: Ukraine transitions from a recipient of donor assistance to a partnership of equals Ukraine MOD 07 May 2026 -- Ukraine is transitioning from receiving donor assistance to an industrial partnership on equal terms and is open to practical forms of cooperation, including joint research and development, localisation of manufacturing, the establishment of joint ventures, and testing new solutions under real combat conditions.
  • Ukraine and its Swedish partners are working to bolster aviation and air defence capabilities. Ukraine MOD 07 May 2026 -- Ukraine and its Swedish partners are working to bolster aviation and air defence capabilities.
  • Barrier-free access during wartime: Ministry of Defence presents inclusive solutions to international partners Ukraine MOD 07 May 2026 -- Ukraine's Ministry of Defence presented partners from NATO member states with advanced solutions in the areas of inclusion and gender equality implemented in the context of the full-scale war.

  • China's J-35 with 0001 fuselage number spotted in media report footage; expert says it may target global market Global Times 07 May 2026 -- A J-35 stealth fighter jet bearing the number "0001" was spotted taxiing out of a hangar in a recent CCTV News program. A Chinese military affairs expert was quoted as saying in the CCTV report on Friday that signs in the footage suggests this particular J-35 could be an export version of the J-35 series, making it a likely candidate for China's next major export fighter.

  • Narwhal submarine fires 2 torpedoes during 8th round of dive tests CNA 07 May 2026 -- As a part of its eighth round of dive tests, the Narwhal, Taiwan's first domestically built submarine, conducted torpedo launch trials, CSBC Corp., Taiwan, the vessel's primary contractor, said Thursday.

  • Allies work as one launching and landing at sea Australia DOD 07 May 2026 -- Four nations, four warships and three of the most sophisticated airborne antisubmarine warfare platforms working as one.
  • Exercise Pitch Black returns in 2026 Australia DOD 07 May 2026 -- Aircraft from around the world will take to Australia's skies for the return of Exercise Pitch Black, the Royal Australian Air Force's premier, biennial flying activity.

  • NZDF concludes Papua New Guinea deployment New Zealand Defence Force 07 May 2026 -- The Royal New Zealand Air Force's (RNZAF) No. 3 Squadron has wrapped up a busy 20 days in Papua New Guinea (PNG), finishing with two days of trooping and air sniper training with PNG and Australian militaries.

  • U.S. House Democrats press Trump administration to disclose Israel's nuclear program People's Daily 07 May 2026 -- Democrats in the U.S. House of Representatives have demanded the Trump administration publicly acknowledge Israel's undeclared nuclear weapons program.
  • Amid war on Iran, US lawmakers demand transparency on Israel's nuclear arsenal Press TV 07 May 2026 -- In a rare move, US lawmakers have pressed the Trump administration to end its official silence on Israel's nuclear arsenal.

  • INS SAGARDHWANI ARRIVES IN CAM RANH, VIETNAM India PIB 07 May 2026 -- INS Sagardhwani, an oceanographic research vessel, arrived at Cam Ranh, Vietnam on 05 May 2026, marking a significant step in India's scientific cooperation with Vietnam.

  • Joining Hands to Build Integrated Support System for Service Members From Self-Development to Career Consulting ROK MND 07 May 2026 -- Defense Minister Ahn Gyu-back, seventh from left, and Cho Il-hoon, seventh from right, president of The Korea Economic Daily, pose for a photo with participants during the "Soldier Encouragement Tomorrow PASS" cooperation MOU signing ceremony at the Ministry of National Defense in Yongsan-gu, Seoul, on April 23.
  • "Will Spare No Effort to Ensure Smooth Implementation of Legislation Essential to Military Development" ROK MND 07 May 2026 -- Defense Minister Ahn Gyu-back responds to questions from National Assembly members during the first plenary session of the National Defense Committee held as part of the 434th extraordinary session on April 14, 2026.

  • Baku hosts working meeting of military cybersecurity specialists from Azerbaijan and Georgia Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 07 May 2026 -- In accordance with the bilateral military cooperation plan for 2026 between the Ministry of Defense of the Republic of Azerbaijan and the Ministry of Defense of Georgia, a working meeting of military cybersecurity specialists from both countries was held in Baku, Azerbaijan's Ministry of Defense told AZERTAC.
  • Defense Ministry: "EFES-2026" multinational exercise continues Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 07 May 2026 -- "EFES-2026" multinational joint exercise, being conducted in the Republic of Türkiye with the participation of a group of servicemen from the Azerbaijan Army, is successfully ongoing, the Ministry of Defense told AZERTAC.

  • Joint Statement by the Nordic Ministers of Defence - Nordic Defence Cooperation Government of Norway 07 May 2026 -- On May 5-6 the Nordic Ministers of Defence gathered in Trøndelag under Norway's chairship of NORDEFCO in 2026.

  • Defence Minister Pevkur in Istanbul: Defence cooperation between Turkey and Estonia intensifies Estonia MoD 07 May 2026 -- Minister of Defence Hanno Pevkur met with his Turkish counterpart Yaşar Güler in Istanbul to discuss closer defence cooperation, the development of the defence industry and the upcoming Ankara Summit.

  • Ministry of Foreign Affairs expresses protest to Russia over drone incident in Latgale this morning Latvia MFA 07 May 2026 -- Today, 7 May 2026, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs summoned the Chargé d'Affaires of the Embassy of Russia to present a note of protest condemning the incident that occurred earlier this morning on the territory of the Republic of Latvia, when several unmanned aerial vehicles entered the municipalities of Rēzekne, Balvi and Ludza from the airspace of the Russian Federation.

  • Poland Ready to Host US Troops Withdrawn From Germany Sputnik 07 May 2026 -- Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell earlier confirmed to RIA Novosti that the Department of War plans to withdraw 5,000 US troops from Germany over the next six to 12 months.

  • MoND leadership's meeting with the Chairman of NATO Military Committee Romania - Ministry of National Defence 07 May 2026 -- Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, and the Chief of Defence Staff, General Gheorghiță Vlad, had a meeting on Thursday, May 7th, at the MoND headquarters, with the Chairman of the NATO Military Committee, Admiral Giuseppe Cavo Dragone, as part of the allied official's visit to Romania.

  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with Holy See Secretary of State Parolin US Dept. of State 07 May 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with Pope Leo XIV US Dept. of State 07 May 2026
  • Joint Statement on the U.S.-Switzerland Strategic Partnership Dialogue US Dept. of State 07 May 2026 -- ccccThe following is the text of a joint statement by the United States of America and the Swiss Confederation on the occasion of the U.S.-Switzerland Strategic Partnership Dialogue.
  • United States Welcomes Paraguay's Signing of the Artemis Accords US Dept. of State 07 May 2026 -- The Department of State congratulates the Republic of Paraguay on joining the Artemis Accords. Paraguay is the 67th country to sign the Accords to date, pledging its commitment to peaceful space activities.

  • Sanctioning Iran-Aligned Actors Undermining Iraq's Sovereignty and Exploiting Its Resources US Dept. of State 07 May 2026 -- The Trump Administration is taking decisive action against individuals and entities that are exploiting Iraq's oil wealth to fund the Iranian regime's terrorism in the region.
  • Economic Fury Targets Iraqi Oil Official, Iran-Backed Terrorist Militias in Iraq US Treasury 07 May 2026 -- Today, the U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) increased economic pressure on Iran and its proxy militias in Iraq by designating individuals and businesses exploiting Iraq's oil sector and undermining the country's security. This action includes Ali Maarij Al-Bahadly,Iraq's Deputy Minister of Oil, who abuses his position to facilitate the diversion of oil to be sold for the benefit of the Iranian regime and its proxy militias in Iraq.

  • 7 May 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 07 May 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • PM Netanyahu on Yesterday's Action in Beirut Israel - Prime Minister 07 May 2026 -- "Yesterday evening, we eliminated the commander of Hezbollah's Radwan Force, in the heart of Beirut."

  • Hamas slams killing of Hayya's son in 'cowardly attack' by Israel on Gaza Press TV 07 May 2026 -- The Palestinian resistance movement Hamas has strongly denounced the killing of Azzam al-Hayya in a "cowardly attack" by Israel on the besieged Gaza Strip, stressing that targeting the families of Palestinian leaders will not disrupt the resistance across the territory.
  • Hamas: Israel adheres to no obligations, ceasefire talks at standstill over broken commitments Press TV 07 May 2026 -- The leader of the Hamas resistance movement in the Gaza Strip says the occupying Israeli regime adheres to no obligations and reneges on all agreements it has already signed.
  • Senior Hamas leader's son wounded, five others killed in Israeli strikes on Gaza: Report Press TV 07 May 2026 -- Israeli airstrikes have injured the son of senior Hamas leader Khalil al-Hayya and killed at least five other people across the Gaza Strip, a report says.

  • Lebanon: Fresh strike on Beirut suburbs 'a very alarming development' UN News 07 May 2026 -- An Israeli airstrike overnight on the southern suburbs of the Lebanese capital Beirut has sparked a new wave of displacement among civilians already impacted by months of conflict, the United Nations said on Thursday.

  • US confirms it will host another round of Israel-Lebanon talks next week Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 07 May 2026 -- The US State Department confirms that it will host the third round of talks between representatives from Israel and Lebanon on May 14 and 15, according to the Times of Israel.

  • Prime Minister Carney speaks with President of the State of Palestine Mahmoud Abbas Prime Minister of Canada 07 May 2026 -- Today, the Prime Minister, Mark Carney, spoke with the President of the State of Palestine, Mahmoud Abbas.
  • The OIC Assistant Secretary-General for Palestine and Al-Quds Affairs discusses with ISESCO ways to support the steadfastness of the Palestinian people Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 07 May 2026
  • Statement MFA Poland - Ministry of Foreign Affairs 07 May 2026 -- The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Poland expresses strong concern regarding another incident involving a soldier of the Israeli Defense Forces (IDF) in Lebanon, who showed disrespect toward the figure of the Virgin Mary. Such actions offend the religious sentiments of Christians.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Sudan UNOCHA 07 May 2026

  • CCG tracks, monitors Philippine aircraft harassing Chinese scientific research vessel in Houteng Jiao in SCS in 'professional, standardized' manner: spokesperson Global Times 07 May 2026 -- On May 6, a Philippine Coast Guard Islander aircraft deliberately approached and harassed China's scientific research vessel Xiangyanghong 33, which was conducting a legitimate marine ecological survey in the waters near China's Houteng Jiao. The China Coast Guard (CCG) tracked and monitored the aircraft and handled the situation in accordance with laws and regulations to ensure safe operations of the Chinese vessel. The CCG's maneuvers were professional and standardized throughout the whole process. The allegations by the Philippine side are mere distortion of facts and intended to mislead the public, CCG spokesperson Jiang Lue said Thursday.
  • PCG monitors illegal Chinese research, militia vessels in KIG waters Philippine News Agency 07 May 2026 -- The Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) monitored the intrusion of Chinese research and militia vessels in waters surrounding the Kalayaan Island Group (KIG) during a routine Maritime Domain Awareness (MDA) flight on Wednesday.

  • Message to the Nation by Deputy Prime Minister/ Foreign Minister Senator Mohammad Ishaq Dar on First Anniversary of Marka-e-Haq Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 07 May 2026

  • Security Council Speakers Urge Sudan, South Sudan to Revive Abyei Political Process amid Rising Violence, Reduced UN Presence on Ground UN Security Council 07 May 2026 -- The conflict in Sudan and instability in South Sudan are delaying agreement on Abyei's final status and degrading conditions in the demilitarized area, United Nations officials warned the Security Council today, as delegates called for the urgent revival of stalled joint oversight mechanisms and diplomatic resolve to advance a durable political solution.
  • UNISFA plays a critical role in protecting civilians and maintaining stability in Abyei: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 07 May 2026 -- Statement by Jess Jambert-Gray, Deputy Political Coordinator, at the UN Security Council meeting on Sudan and South Sudan.

News Reports

  • Filipino man tells RFA how he spied for China, military says there are more Radio Free Asia 07 May 2026 -- Desperate for a new source of income because his business was failing, a Filipino man in his mid-20s answered an online advertisement in 2024 for what he thought was a U.S.-based publication looking for a writer focusing on Philippine defense.

  • FEMA Review Council Releases Final Report DHS 07 May 2026 -- The President's Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) Review Council today released its Final Report, marking a major milestone in the Administration's ongoing efforts to strengthen FEMA's mission, operations, and accountability.

  • Michigan Man Sentenced to 20 years in Prison for Attempting to Provide Material Support to ISIS and Possessing a Destructive Device US Dept. of Justice 07 May 2026 -- A Michigan man was sentenced today to 20 years in prison after having been convicted by a jury last year on two charges of attempting to provide material support to the Islamic State of Iraq and al-Sham, commonly known as ISIS, a designated foreign terrorist organization, and for being a felon in possession of a destructive device, announced Assistant Attorney General for National Security John A. Eisenberg, U.S. Attorney Jerome F. Gorgon Jr. for the Eastern District of Michigan, and Special Agent in Charge Jennifer Runyan of the FBI Detroit Field Office.

  • WHO's response to hantavirus cases linked to a cruise ship WHO 07 May 2026 -- Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General, briefed media today on a cluster of hantavirus cases linked to a cruise ship, the MV Hondius.
  • Hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship not 'another COVID', WHO says UN News 07 May 2026 -- A deadly hantavirus outbreak aboard a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean poses a low global public health risk and is "not the start of another COVID pandemic", the World Health Organization (WHO) said on Thursday.

  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's telephone conversation with Foreign Minister of Oman Badr bin Hamad Al Busaidi Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 07 May 2026
  • NZ announces sanctions on malicious Russian cyber actors, online platforms New Zealand Government 07 May 2026 -- Foreign Minister Winston Peters has announced a new round of sanctions targeting malicious cyber actors and others supporting Russia's illegal war of aggression against Ukraine.
  • Vladimir Putin And Victory Day: A Scaled-Down Parade, A Diminished President? RFE/RL 07 May 2026 -- Over a quarter-century in power, President Vladimir Putin has used Russia's Victory Day parade to show off its military might, accentuate his dominance, and deliver belligerent narratives on World War II and the current geopolitical landscape, often suggesting that Moscow is fighting off a threat from the West that mirrors that of Nazi Germany.

  • Two former Chinese defense ministers handed death sentence with reprieve for graft Global Times 07 May 2026 -- Two former Chinese defense ministers, Wei Fenghe and Li Shangfu, were both sentenced to death with a two-year reprieve over graft charges on Thursday.
  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Lin Jian's Regular Press Conference on May 7, 2026 PRC MofA 07 May 2026
  • Wang Yi Meets with Delegation from the Senate of the Congress of the United States PRC MofA 07 May 2026 -- On May 7, 2026, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Foreign Minister Wang Yi met with a visiting delegation from the Senate of the Congress of the United States (U.S.) led by Senator Steve Daines in Beijing.
  • China's Tianzhou-9 cargo craft undocks from space station combination People's Daily 07 May 2026 -- The Tianzhou-9 cargo craft, tasked with carrying supplies for China's space station, undocked from the station combination at 4:34 p.m. Wednesday (Beijing Time) and switched to independent flight, according to the China Manned Space Agency (CMSA).

  • FM spokesperson responds to speculation over whether Taiwan question is Chinese side's top concern as China-US leaders' meeting approaches Global Times 07 May 2026 -- Taiwan question is at the core of China's core interests and the foundation of the political foundation of China-US relations. Abiding by the one-China principle and the three China-US joint communiqués, and honoring the commitments made by successive US administrations on the Taiwan question, are international obligations the US side must fulfill. They are also a necessary prerequisite for the stable, sound, and sustainable development of China-US relations, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian said on Thursday.
  • China strongly condemns Israeli lawmakers' Taiwan visit as 'despicable,' warns against crossing red lines Press TV 07 May 2026 -- China has issued a strong condemnation of a recent visit by some Knesset members to Taiwan, calling their actions "despicable" and warning that there will be a " price " for crossing China's red lines.
  • Lai Ching-te's trip to Eswatini shows "Taiwan independence" separatism is just shady business: spokesperson People's Daily 07 May 2026 -- The fact that Taiwan region's leader Lai Ching-te sneaked in and out of Eswatini shows exactly that "Taiwan independence" separatism is just shady business and unacceptable to the international community, Chinese foreign ministry spokesperson Lin Jian said on Wednesday, calling his act nothing but a scandalous stunt.
  • Die-hard Taiwan secessionists, relatives not allowed to profit from mainland: spokesperson People's Daily 07 May 2026 -- The Chinese mainland will not allow die-hard "Taiwan independence" secessionists or their relatives to invest in or profit from business activities on the mainland, a spokesperson said on Wednesday.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 07 May 2026

  • Press Statement of Permanent Representative of DPRK to UN KCNA 07 May 2026 -- Kim Song, permanent representative of the DPRK to the UN, issued the following press statement on May 6:

  • Spokesperson's Press Briefing (May 7, 2026) ROK MFA 07 May 2026

  • Iran - Deterioration in Ms Narges Mohammadi's State of health France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 07 May 2026

  • The Executive Order of May 1st and the Blockade Measures Announced Today Further Increase the Harm to the Cuban Population and Reinforce the Threat of Aggression Cuba Ministry of Foreign Affairs 07 May 2026 -- "The Ministry of Foreign Affairs rejects, in the strongest possible terms, the Executive Order issued by the White House on May 1st, 2026, which tightens, to unprecedented and extreme levels, the economic, commercial and financial blockade against Cuba."
  • U.S. Sanctions Target Cuba's Military Regime, Elites US Dept. of State 07 May 2026 -- The Trump Administration is taking decisive action to protect U.S. national security and deprive Cuba's communist regime and military of access to illicit assets.
  • United States must end "energy starvation" of Cuba with severe human rights impacts: UN experts OHCHR 07 May 2026 -- The January 2026 executive order issued by the President of the United States imposing a fuel blockade on Cuba amounts to 'energy starvation' with grave consequences for human rights and the country's overall development, UN experts* warned today.
  • Deputy Prime Minister Radosław Sikorski meets with Cuban opposition leader José Daniel Ferrer Poland - Ministry of Foreign Affairs 07 May 2026 -- On 7 May, Polish Foreign Minister Radosław Sikorski met in Warsaw with José Daniel Ferrer, one of Cuba's key opposition figures and the founder of the Patriotic Union of Cuba (UNPACU). His travel to Europe is another step in Mr Ferrer's peaceful struggle for Cuba to become a democratic state.

  • Transcript of Weekly Media Briefing by the Official Spokesperson (May 07, 2026) India MEA 07 May 2026
  • Dr. Jitendra Singh holds bilateral meeting with French Minister India-France ties set for expansion in space, AI and emerging technologies India PIB 07 May 2026 -- Union Minister of State (Independent Charge) for Science & Technology, Earth Sciences, and Minister of State for PMO, Personnel, Public Grievances, Pensions, Atomic Energy and Space, Dr. Jitendra Singh today held a bilateral meeting via video conference with Prof. Philippe Baptiste, France's Minister for Higher Education, Research and Space, reviewing the expanding canvas of India-France cooperation in science, technology and space.

  • Transcript of the Weekly Press Briefing by the Spokesperson on Thursday, 07th May 2026 Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 07 May 2026

  • The Daily Press Briefing (05/07/2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 07 May 2026
  • France risks undermining Kanak Indigenous People's self-determination in New Caledonia, UN committee warns OHCHR 07 May 2026 -- France must ensure that political and constitutional reforms concerning New Caledonia proceed only with the effective participation of the Kanak Indigenous People, grounded in their free, prior and informed consent, the UN Committee on the Elimination of Racial Discrimination warned today.

  • Prime Minister Carney speaks with President of the Republic of Korea Lee Jae Myung Prime Minister of Canada 07 May 2026 -- Today, the Prime Minister, Mark Carney, spoke with the President of the Republic of Korea, Lee Jae Myung.

  • Political Consultations Between the Foreign Ministries of Armenia and Finland Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Armenia 07 May 2026 -- On May 6, on the margins of the "Yerevan Dialogue" international forum, political consultations took place between the Ministries of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Armenia and the Republic of Finland. The consultations were led by Vahan Kostanyan, Deputy Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Armenia, and Sari Rautio, Director General of the Euro-Atlantic Department at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Finland.

  • US Envoy Volz: Balkan Energy Security A National Security Priority For Washington RFE/RL 07 May 2026 -- The United States sees energy security in the Western Balkans as a national-security priority and is stepping up efforts to help the region reduce its dependence on Russian supplies, Joshua Volz, a special envoy for energy integration at the US Department of Energy, told RFE/RL.

  • World News in Brief: Somalia drought response, Gaza and Ukraine aid updates, human rights abuses in Tunisia UN News 07 May 2026 -- In Somalia's Puntland region, dried out watering holes, animal carcasses and old pots filled with ash have become part of the landscape as worsening drought conditions deepen a growing hunger crisis.
